assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.

commonMistakes: Memorizing syntax without predicting the underlying value or execution order.
Mutating shared data when the task expects a new value or explicit ownership.
Treating browser APIs and language features as the same layer.
Choosing an abstraction from habit rather than required operations.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Explain call stack using a new example and justify the result.
Apply web apis using a new example and justify the result.
Explain callback and microtask queues using a new example and justify the result.
Trace and explain event-loop scheduling using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Call stack** → **Web APIs** → **Callback and microtask queues** → **Event-loop scheduling**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the The Event Loop and Async Execution rule and the observable result.
choices.label: Promise reactions use the microtask queue; timers and many events use task queues. Microtasks run after the stack clears and before the next task.

choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the The Event Loop and Async Execution rule and the observable result.
choices.label: The event loop waits for the stack to clear, drains microtasks, then advances to another task. A zero-delay timer means no earlier than a future task.

choices.feedback: The call stack records active execution contexts. Calls push frames and returns pop them. Long synchronous work blocks later JavaScript and delays queued work.
choices.label: The call stack records active execution contexts. Calls push frames and returns pop them. Long synchronous work blocks later JavaScript and delays queued work.

choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the The Event Loop and Async Execution rule and the observable result.

sections.body: The call stack records active execution contexts. Calls push frames and returns pop them. Long synchronous work blocks later JavaScript and delays queued work.
sections.title: Call stack

sections.body: Browser APIs perform work outside the JavaScript call stack and queue callbacks or promise reactions when results are ready.

sections.body: Promise reactions use the microtask queue; timers and many events use task queues. Microtasks run after the stack clears and before the next task.

sections.body: The event loop waits for the stack to clear, drains microtasks, then advances to another task. A zero-delay timer means no earlier than a future task.

summary: The Event Loop and Async Execution connects Call stack, Web APIs, Callback and microtask queues.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: The Event Loop and Async Execution

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: This topic turns the event loop and async execution from isolated terminology into a practical tool for reading, writing, and reviewing code. It belongs to the **Asynchronous JavaScript** module.
